senses.definition: to fall back into a previous bad habit or condition after an attempt to stop or improve
examples: After years of sobriety, he relapsed and started drinking again.
The patient relapsed shortly after the treatment ended.

senses.definition: able to resist or prevent relapse; not prone to returning to a previous bad state or habit
examples: The relapseproof program helps participants maintain long-term sobriety.
They sought relapseproof strategies to stay on track.

senses.definition: a person who relapses or falls back into a previous bad habit or condition
examples: He is an occasional relapser, but he keeps trying to quit.
The clinic supports both relapser and non-relapser participants.

senses.definition: plural of relapser; people who relapse
examples: Relapsers were given additional support and counseling.
The program tracks relapsers to tailor interventions.

senses.definition: instances of returning to a previous bad habit or illness after a period of improvement
examples: Relapses are common in the early weeks after quitting smoking.
They monitored relapses to adjust treatment.

senses.definition: falling back into a previous bad habit or illness after a period of improvement (present participle)
examples: He is relapsing after months of sobriety.
Relapsing symptoms appeared during the winter.

senses.definition: to place again; to put back in a previous position
examples: They decided to relast the items in the order they were found.
Relast the shelves after cleaning.

senses.definition: one who relasts; a person who places items back in position
examples: The relaster organized the tools.
A skilled relaster can speed up assembly lines.

senses.definition: plural of relating; items that relate to something; in philosophy, things that are related
examples: The relata of the theory are objects and their properties.
In discussions, the relata must be clearly defined.

senses.definition: the quality of being easy to relate to or understand
examples: Relatability is important for connecting with an audience.
Her relatability makes her talk engaging.

senses.definition: easy to relate to or understand; familiar and realistic
examples: Her story is relatable to many students.
The characters feel relatable and real.

senses.definition: to latch again; to fasten with a latch once more
examples: Relatch the door after you leave.
They relatch the gate to keep it secure.

senses.definition: to tell a story or give information about something; to show or connect
examples: She could relate the events in detail.
This data relate to our previous findings.

senses.definition: connected by a relationship or belonging to the same family or topic
examples: They are related by blood.
Those books are related to the topic we are studying.

senses.definition: in a way that shows a relation to something else; with regard to the topic mentioned
examples: Relatedly, the study also considered environmental factors.
Relatedly, the authors discuss the ethical implications.

senses.definition: the quality or state of being related or connected
examples: The relatedness between the two theories is strong.
Researchers study the relatedness of concepts.

senses.definition: plural of relatedness; multiple instances of relatedness
examples: The study compared the relatednesses across populations.
Different relatednesses can affect interpretation.

senses.definition: a person who relates something or tells a story; a narrator
examples: She was the main relater of the events.
As a relater, he knows the full sequence of facts.

senses.definition: plural of relater; people who relate or narrate
examples: The relaters shared their experiences at the conference.
Different relaters offered different perspectives.

senses.definition: is connected or has a relation to
examples: This fact relates to our earlier discussion.
The chapter relates several stories to a common theme.

senses.definition: present participle of relate; the act of showing a connection
examples: Relating the incident to the bigger picture helps understanding.
She was relating the two sets of data.

senses.definition: the process of making something into a relation or into a relational form; a linguistic or technical term in some fields
examples: The relatinization of the data improved interpretability.
Scholars discussed the relatinization process in the model.

senses.definition: a connection or association between things or people; a relationship
examples: What is the relation between these two ideas?
The relation between cause and effect is clear.

senses.definition: concerned with or involving relationships; describing how things relate to each other
examples: Relational databases store data in tables linked by keys.
Relational thinking helps solve complex problems.

senses.definition: the quality of being related or the state of having relationships; the relational nature of things
examples: Relationality is central to social theory.
They discussed the relationality between language and culture.
